head_sha: CORRECTED (orchestrator, codex CONCERN #1): worktree branch HEAD was 1492f46 (not 639d04c); landed on main via cherry-pick as 2ef8c3d (RED) -> 64f72e0 (GREEN/removal) -> 7b6965e (docs+0.8.x stub) -> d2c0bf4 (this artifact), then an orchestrator remediation commit fixing the stale drain_embedder_events comment (codex CONCERN #2).
removed_symbols: commit_projection_outcomes auto-drift block (observed_vectors plumbing, pinned_this_tx flag, projected-tracker cos/debounce/armed check, 200k-cap branch, post-commit tracker fold)
kept_symbols: cosine_similarity (now used only by the manual recompute report)
MeanRecomputeTrigger (single Manual variant kept; DriftAuto removed)
trigger_enum_decision: Removed the DriftAuto variant (the spec's preferred option; it was unconstructed after the carve-out and is unpushed/internal). Kept the MeanRecomputeTrigger enum itself with its single Manual variant rather than collapsing it to a unit, so reviving the 0.8.x auto path is a purely additive re-introduction of a variant + tag. The CLI/py/napi `trigger` surfaces are unchanged for Manual.
red_test: topic_pivot_does_not_auto_recompute_mid_ingest -- pins a topic-A-skewed mean, floods topic-B past the old debounce window, and asserts the engine emits NO MeanVecRecomputed (DriftAuto / any) and leaves mean_vec byte-identical. Guards against the automatic in-ingest mean-recompute ever returning. RED-FAILED on pre-removal code: the auto detector fired twice (MeanVecRecomputed{DriftAuto} @ doc_count 352 and 608). GREEN after the carve-out.
harness_seam_cleanup: EU7_SUPPRESS_AUTO_RECOMPUTE / set_mean_recompute_dynamic_max_for_test were NOT present in eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs on this base -- S1 fix-1 (codex BLOCK) replaced the env-gated seam with set_search_limit_for_test, so there was no suppress block to remove. EU7_FORCE_FULL_RECOMPUTE (which calls the MANUAL engine.recompute_mean()) is untouched and still compiles. The only consumers of set_mean_recompute_dynamic_max_for_test were the two removed pr2b auto-path tests; the seam itself is now removed from the engine.
notes: cargo build -p fathomdb-engine -p fathomdb-embedder -p fathomdb-cli (also -p fathomdb-py -p fathomdb-napi for the ripple) is clean with no warnings. cargo test -p fathomdb-engine is green except ac_007b_slow_threshold_reconfigurable in lifecycle_observability.rs -- a pre-existing wall-clock flake (the 'slow' CTE finishes under the 500ms threshold on this dev box so no slow-statement signal fires); it FAILS IDENTICALLY on the clean baseline (stash-verified) and touches none of the mean-centering surface. cargo test -p fathomdb-cli and -p fathomdb-embedder fully green; the doctor recompute-mean happy-path/--json + lock-held tests pass. eu7/eu8 long harnesses compile and self-skip without AGENT_LONG, confirming the harness still builds against the carved engine. Manual recompute path correctness/crash-atomicity unchanged (reuses run_pin_and_requantize_pass; events post-durable-commit; commit_gate preserved); initial pin behavior unchanged. NOT pushed.
